frameworks.name: explicit conditioning observer model
frameworks.one_line: decision outcome stored as extra bit and combined with intact stimulus representation in downstream inference

frameworks.name: implicit conditioning
frameworks.one_line: decision directly modifies working-memory representation, erasing decision-inconsistent information

frameworks.name: self-consistent Bayesian post-decision inference
frameworks.one_line: biases arise from conditioning percepts on preceding categorical decisions

core_question: Does a categorical decision modify the stimulus representation in working memory, or does it act as an additional explicit cue at the inference stage?
key_assumptions: subjects maintain consistency between categorical decision and subsequent estimate
stimulus uncertainty differentially predicts implicit vs explicit conditioning signatures
incorrect-decision trials are diagnostic of the conditioning mechanism
manipulations: two-stage task: categorical CW/CCW judgment then orientation reconstruction
key_findings: estimation biases on incorrect-decision trials scale with stimulus uncertainty
implicit-conditioning model predicts insensitivity to uncertainty, inconsistent with data
explicit-conditioning observer model closely predicts the measured biases
interpretations: categorical decisions do not overwrite the working-memory stimulus representation
decision outcome is retained as a separate bit and integrated downstream during estimation
